title: Quadlet: When terminating a container via sigterm fails sigkill stops network connectivity for the entire pod
body:
### Issue Description

When a container inside a pod is terminated via sigkill all process of the pod seem to be terminated as well.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

How to reproduce
1. Setup two quadlets setup using the same pod.
2. Setup user network via slirp4netns
3. Make sure one of the containers cannot be terminated via sigterm


### Describe the results you received

E.g. slirp4netns is getting killed:
Jan 09 15:43:52 HOSTNAME systemd[989]: bfd.service: Killing process 1215 (slirp4netns) with signal SIGKILL.

Stoping the pod fails with
```
WARN[0010] StopSignal SIGTERM failed to stop container bfd-frontend-dev in 10 seconds, resorting to SIGKILL
Error: stopping container 0e451318d8100bb101a0426b6e2b88117021e069bb67f6a05bc6457c0d517cf6: container 0e451318d8100bb101a0426b6e2b88117021e069bb67f6a05bc6457c0d517cf6 conmon exited prematurely, exit code could not be retrieved: internal libpod error
```

### Describe the results you expected

Expected:
When other containers are running in a pod, pod processes should not get killed.

### podman info output

```yaml
Linux: Red Hat Enterprise Linux release 9.5
Version: 4.9.4-rhel
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
Upstream only support the latest version so please use podman 5.3.

Prior to 5.0 quadlet had no pod support at all. With 5.0 or newer you must setup and configure the pod via quadlet then this should work properly.
